title: Aion Store libSQL — The Default Durable Event Store
intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
problem: The EventStore contract (AC-005) is precise but unimplemented for durability; InMemoryStore (AC-006) satisfies it behaviourally but holds everything in process memory and is the oracle, not a deployable backend. Every real deployment that does not bring its own backend needs durable persistence, and the design names libSQL as that default.

Atomicity under a sequence guard is load-bearing: append must apply a batch all-or-none, gated on an expected sequence, rejecting and writing nothing if the stored head moved on. Over SQL this is one transaction reading the head, comparing, and either inserting every event or rolling back; a partial write or a head read outside the transaction corrupts replay integrity silently.

The typed contract must translate faithfully to SQL: read_history, list_active, query, schedule_timer, and expired_timers map onto an append-only events table and a durable timers table. query must translate a WorkflowFilter (type, status, time range, parent) into a WHERE clause without leaking SQL across the trait boundary, and WorkflowStatus is a projection over events (CO7) the store must derive exactly as the oracle does — never a stored status field that drifts.

Two deployment shapes must come from one backend: libSQL's SDK offers a local file database and an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary. The crate exposes both — zero-infra embedded and embedded-replica sync for the distributed path — through one config type, without the engine caring which is in use.

Built ad hoc — its own notion of an event, loose atomicity, its own status logic — the crate desynchronises from the oracle and every other backend. It must be a transcription of the AC contract into durable storage, verified against the AC-007 conformance suite as its correctness oracle.

solution.summary: One crate, aion-store-libsql, depending only on aion-store (and through it aion-core) plus the external libsql SDK. It provides LibSqlStore: an impl EventStore over libSQL. Schema is two tables created idempotently on open: events (workflow_id, seq, event blob, recorded_at; PK (workflow_id, seq)) as the append-only log, and timers (workflow_id, timer_id, fire_at; PK (workflow_id, timer_id)) so re-scheduling replaces (idempotent under replay). Events are stored as serialised blobs — the store is type-erased and never interprets payloads. append opens a transaction, reads the head seq, compares to the expected sequence, and either inserts the whole batch with contiguous seqs and commits, or rolls back returning SequenceConflict and writing nothing; the guard and inserts are one transaction so racing writers resolve to one commit and one conflict. WorkflowStatus and summaries derive from lifecycle event rows via aion-core's projection, never a mutable status column. A Deserialize-only LibSqlConfig selects embedded (local file) or embedded-replica (local file + remote primary URL + token) mode and surfaces libSQL WAL/sync settings with no hardcoded defaults. Correctness oracle is the shared AC-007 run_event_store_suite, run against LibSqlStore over a temp file as a real test; persistence-across-reopen tests cover the durability axis the oracle cannot; only embedded-replica sync tests are runtime env-gated on AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL. libSQL is production-ready today; Turso-native (Limbo) is beta, and the trait abstraction makes swapping to it later a drop-in.

decisions.title: LibSqlStore implements the AC EventStore trait unchanged
decisions.choice: This crate provides only an impl EventStore over libSQL. The trait, StoreError, InMemoryStore, and the conformance suite are cluster AC, consumed here. The crate depends on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) and no other Aion crate. The engine talks to Arc<dyn EventStore> and cannot distinguish this backend from the oracle except that it persists.

decisions.title: Events stored as opaque serialised blobs, not exploded columns
decisions.choice: The events table stores the serialised Event as a blob plus only the columns the store must filter/order on (workflow_id, seq, recorded_at, plus summary fields read from lifecycle rows). The store is type-erased per AC's D2 — it persists and reads back Events and never interprets payloads. Rejected exploding events into typed columns: it would couple the schema to the Event variant set and break type-erasure.

decisions.title: Summaries derived from lifecycle event rows; no mutable workflow record
decisions.choice: query and list_active derive workflow type, status, start, and end by reading the relevant lifecycle event rows (WorkflowStarted carries type+start; the terminal event carries status+end), computing WorkflowStatus via aion-core's projection. Rejected a mutable status column updated per append: it duplicates authority belonging to the event log and is the classic source of status/history skew, violating CO7.

decisions.title: Sequence guard enforced inside the append transaction
decisions.choice: append opens one libSQL transaction, reads the head seq for the workflow, compares the expected next sequence, and either inserts the whole batch with contiguous seqs and commits or rolls back and returns SequenceConflict, writing nothing. The head read and inserts are one atomic unit, so two writers racing on the same expected sequence resolve to exactly one commit and one conflict via transactional isolation. Rejected a pre-flight head read then a separate insert (TOCTOU race) and rejected relying solely on PK uniqueness to surface conflicts (conflates sequence conflict with other constraint violations, risks partial batch).

decisions.title: One config type, both modes; not two store types
decisions.choice: LibSqlStore is a single type. Its constructor takes a Deserialize-only LibSqlConfig whose enum variant selects embedded (local file) or embedded-replica (local file + remote primary URL + auth token) mode, and which surfaces libSQL WAL/sync durability settings with no hardcoded defaults (CO10). The engine sees one store regardless of mode. Rejected a separate LibSqlReplicaStore: it would duplicate the entire EventStore impl for a difference that is only how the connection is opened.

decisions.title: Target libSQL now; Turso-native is a future drop-in
decisions.choice: The from-scratch Rust rewrite (Turso Database, formerly Limbo) is beta as of mid-2026 and not production-ready; libSQL (C fork + Rust SDK) is production-ready today, so this crate targets libSQL. Because aion-store abstracts the backend, swapping libSQL internals for Turso-native — or adding a sibling aion-store-turso — later is a drop-in with zero engine impact. Recorded so the choice is deliberate and revisitable.

decisions.title: Conformance suite is the oracle; durability tests cover the rest
decisions.choice: The crate's correctness oracle is the shared AC-007 run_event_store_suite, run against LibSqlStore over a temporary local file as a real runtime test (embedded mode needs no service, so never gated). Persistence-across-reopen tests cover the one axis the in-memory oracle structurally cannot: append, drop, reopen the same file, assert history/active-list/timers intact. Only embedded-replica sync tests are runtime env-gated on AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL (skip line + Ok when unset), never #[ignore].

decisions.title: Schema created idempotently on open via embedded migration
decisions.choice: Opening a store runs idempotent DDL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) for the events and timers tables and their indexes, so a fresh file and an existing file both arrive at the same schema without an external migration tool. events PK is (workflow_id, seq); timers PK is (workflow_id, timer_id) so re-scheduling replaces (idempotent under replay); fire_at is indexed for expired_timers range scans.

decisions.title: Foreign errors mapped into StoreError at the boundary
decisions.choice: All libsql driver errors and serde (de)serialisation failures are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy (Backend, Serialization, and SequenceConflict/NotFound where the contract specifies) at the crate boundary. No libsql or serde error type leaks across the EventStore surface, so the engine handles one uniform error surface regardless of backend.

constraints.description: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

goals: LibSqlStore implements every EventStore method over libSQL: append, read_history, list_active, query, schedule_timer, expired_timers.

Schema: append-only events table (workflow_id, seq, event blob, recorded_at; PK (workflow_id, seq)) and durable timers table (workflow_id, timer_id, fire_at; PK (workflow_id, timer_id)), created idempotently on open.
append applies a batch atomically with the expected-sequence guard in one transaction; mismatch returns SequenceConflict and writes nothing.
read_history returns the full ordered history; list_active returns non-terminal workflows via the status projection; query translates WorkflowFilter to SQL.
schedule_timer persists a durable timer (re-schedule replaces); expired_timers returns timers due as of a given instant.
WorkflowStatus and WorkflowSummary derive from lifecycle event rows via aion-core's projection, never a mutable status column.
LibSqlConfig (Deserialize-only) selects embedded (local file) or embedded-replica (local + remote primary + token) mode and surfaces WAL/sync settings with no hardcoded defaults.
LibSqlStore passes the shared AC-007 conformance suite over a temporary local file as a real runtime test.
Persistence-across-reopen tests prove history, active list, and timers survive close and reopen.
Embedded-replica sync is implemented and exercised by tests runtime-gated on AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL (skip line + Ok when unset), never #[ignore].
All libsql and serde errors map into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary.
non_goals: No new EventStore trait, StoreError, InMemoryStore, or conformance suite — those are cluster AC, consumed here unchanged.
No PostgreSQL backend — that is cluster AX.
No engine, replay, timer-firing, or scheduling behaviour — the store persists and retrieves; expired_timers returns due timers, it does not fire them (engine is AE, durability/replay is AD).
No Turso-native (Limbo) backend — libSQL today; Turso is a future drop-in swap once it leaves beta (decision D6).
No event compaction or snapshotting — a later cross-backend concern once history size is a measured problem.
No wire/gRPC concerns — the store reads/writes serialised Event blobs; the network protocol is aion-proto (cluster AW).

structure.crates/aion-store-libsql/src/append.rs: [AS-004] atomic append with the sequence guard
structure.crates/aion-store-libsql/tests/conforman: [AS-007] runs the AC-007 suite against LibSqlStore
